era_context: Identical to previous years: Old Kingdom Egypt (Dynasty VI) under Pepi II, Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin, Indus Valley Civilization in mature phase. This is a period of established empires before the disruptions of the late 23rd century BCE.

events.title: Egypt under Pepi II: administrative continuity
events.description: Pepi II's reign continues with no documented specific events for this year. The Egyptian state functions as a centralized monarchy with provincial administration. This year falls within the period of gradual institutional change that will eventually lead to the First Intermediate Period.

sources.name: Administrative records and inscriptions from Dynasty VI

events.certainty_note: No specific events are documented for -2349. The year falls within Pepi II's reign based on traditional chronology.

events.title: Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin: military and administrative dominance
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues with the Akkadian Empire at its territorial maximum. The empire maintains control over vast territories through military force and administrative centralization. No specific events are documented for this year.

sources.name: Akkadian royal inscriptions and administrative records

events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but no specific events are documented for -2349.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events are documented for -2349, making it impossible to identify disconfirming evidence.
historiographic_note: The year -2349 is not individually documented in surviving sources. Historical understanding is based on broader patterns of the era.
